content: **Company:** Northstar Analytics, Inc. **Version:** 1.0 **Effective Date:** July 1, 2026
Northstar Analytics expects all employees to act ethically, respectfully, and professionally. This Code of Conduct defines the standards that guide our workplace behavior and business decisions.
Employees must treat coworkers, clients, partners, and vendors with courtesy and respect. Harassment, intimidation, discrimination, retaliation, and abusive behavior are not acceptable.
Employees must provide accurate information, act honestly in business matters, and avoid misleading statements, falsified records, or deceptive conduct.

Employees must avoid situations in which personal interests interfere, or appear to interfere, with the interests of the company. Any actual or potential conflict of interest must be disclosed to management or Human Resources.
Employees must protect confidential company, employee, and customer information. Confidential material may not be disclosed, copied, or used for personal gain.
Northstar Analytics prohibits harassment of any kind, including verbal, written, visual, physical, or online misconduct. Employees who witness or experience harassment should report it immediately.
Employees must use company time, systems, funds, and materials responsibly and only for legitimate business purposes, except where limited personal use is explicitly permitted.

Employees are encouraged to report concerns about unethical conduct, policy violations, fraud, harassment, or safety issues. Reports may be made to a manager, Human Resources, or any approved reporting channel.
No employee will be penalized for reporting a concern in good faith or participating in an investigation.
Violations of this Code of Conduct may lead to coaching, written warnings, suspension, or termination depending on the seriousness of the issue.
**Company:** Northstar Analytics, Inc. **Version:** 1.0 **Effective Date:** July 1, 2026

Welcome to Northstar Analytics, Inc. This handbook provides a general overview of our workplace expectations, employee benefits, communication norms, and day-to-day policies. It is intended to help employees understand how we work together as a professional, respectful, and accountable organization.

Northstar Analytics classifies employees as full-time, part-time, temporary, or contract workers. - **Full-time employees** are scheduled to work 40 hours per week and are generally eligible for the full benefits package. - **Part-time employees** work fewer than 30 hours per week and may receive limited benefits depending on local law and company policy. - **Temporary employees** are hired for a limited project or timeframe. - **Contract workers** are not company employees and are managed according to vendor or contract terms.

Standard business hours are **9:00 a.m. to 5:30 p.m., Monday through Friday**, with a 30-minute unpaid lunch break. Employees are expected to be available and responsive during their assigned working hours unless approved otherwise.

Employees are expected to report to work on time and maintain reliable attendance. If an employee will be late or absent, they must notify their direct manager as early as possible and no later than **30 minutes before the start of the workday**. Unplanned absences of more than **three consecutive workdays** may require supporting documentation.

Northstar Analytics is committed to equal opportunity and does not tolerate discrimination based on race, color, religion, sex, gender identity, sexual orientation, national origin, age, disability, or any other protected status. We are committed to a workplace where employees are treated with dignity and respect.

Employees should use company communication tools professionally and respectfully. Important work decisions should be documented in writing through approved channels such as email, internal chat, project management systems, or meeting notes.

Employees participate in a formal performance review process **twice per year**, typically in June and December. Managers are expected to provide regular feedback, discuss goals, and identify professional development opportunities.

Employees must act professionally, honestly, and respectfully in all business interactions. Bullying, harassment, threats, dishonesty, misuse of company property, or violation of company policy may result in disciplinary action, up to and including termination.

Company laptops, systems, email accounts, and collaboration platforms are provided for business purposes. Limited personal use may be permitted if it does not interfere with work, create security risks, or violate company policy.

Employees may have access to confidential business information, customer records, financial information, product strategies, and internal operational data. Confidential information must not be disclosed to unauthorized persons during or after employment.

Employees must follow all workplace safety instructions and report unsafe conditions immediately. Any work-related injury, safety concern, or security incident must be reported to a manager or Human Resources as soon as possible.

This handbook summarizes major employment expectations, but more detailed rules may appear in separate policy documents such as the Vacation Policy, Remote Work Policy, IT Security Policy, Code of Conduct, and Expense Reimbursement Policy.
**Company:** Northstar Analytics, Inc. **Version:** 1.0 **Effective Date:** July 1, 2026
This policy explains which business expenses may be reimbursed and how employees should submit reimbursement requests.

Reasonable, necessary, and business-related expenses may be reimbursed when properly documented. Eligible expenses may include: - approved business travel, - lodging, - meals during business travel, - local transportation, - client meeting expenses, - approved office supplies purchased for business use.

The following are generally not reimbursable unless expressly approved in advance: - personal entertainment, - traffic fines or parking penalties, - alcohol not connected to an approved client event, - expenses for spouses, family members, or guests, - upgrades for personal convenience.
Any single expense over **$250** requires manager pre-approval unless it is booked through an approved company process. Travel outside the employee's normal work region also requires prior approval.
Employees must submit itemized receipts for any expense of **$25 or more**. Missing receipts may result in delayed processing or denial unless an exception is approved.
Reimbursement requests must be submitted within **30 calendar days** of the expense date. Late submissions may be denied unless there is a documented business reason for the delay.

Employees must submit reimbursement requests through the approved finance or expense management system. Each request should include: - date of expense, - amount, - business purpose, - category, - supporting receipt where required.
Approved reimbursements are typically paid within **10 business days** after final approval and complete documentation.
Submitting false, inflated, duplicate, or misleading expense claims is considered misconduct and may result in disciplinary action.
Questions about this policy should be directed to Finance or Human Resources.
**Company:** Northstar Analytics, Inc. **Version:** 1.0 **Effective Date:** July 1, 2026
This policy establishes the minimum information security requirements for protecting company systems, customer data, confidential information, and digital assets.
This policy applies to all employees, contractors, interns, and temporary staff who access company systems, devices, or data.

All employees must use strong passwords that: - contain at least **12 characters**, - include a mix of uppercase letters, lowercase letters, numbers, and symbols, - are not reused across personal and business accounts. Passwords must be changed immediately if compromise is suspected.
Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is required for all company email, cloud platforms, and business-critical applications where supported.

Employees must use company-approved devices for business work unless written authorization is granted. Devices must be protected with screen locks, antivirus tools, current security updates, and full-disk encryption where configured by IT.

Employees must remain alert to suspicious emails, links, attachments, and social engineering attempts. Suspected phishing messages should be reported to IT immediately and should not be opened, forwarded, or replied to.

Confidential or sensitive information may only be stored, transmitted, or shared using approved company systems. Employees must not upload confidential files to personal storage accounts or unapproved third-party tools.

When working remotely, employees must avoid unsecured public Wi-Fi unless using a company-approved VPN. Sensitive work should not be conducted in a way that exposes company information to unauthorized viewing.
Employees may access only the systems and information necessary for their job responsibilities. Sharing passwords or allowing another person to use one's account is strictly prohibited.
Any suspected security incident, including lost devices, phishing attempts, malware, unauthorized access, or accidental data exposure, must be reported to IT within **one hour of discovery**.
Violations of this policy may result in loss of system access, disciplinary action, or termination, depending on severity.
**Company:** Northstar Analytics, Inc. **Version:** 1.0 **Effective Date:** July 1, 2026
Northstar Analytics supports flexible work arrangements where business needs allow. This policy explains eligibility, availability, and expectations for remote work.

Remote work is available only for roles that can be effectively performed off-site without reducing service quality, security, or team coordination. Eligibility is determined by management based on job function, performance history, and operational requirements.
The standard remote work arrangement is **up to three remote days per week** for eligible employees. Team leaders may require specific in-office collaboration days.

Employees must submit a remote work request to their manager. The request should describe: - the proposed work schedule, - the primary remote work location, - internet reliability, - availability during business hours. Final approval requires manager confirmation and may be reviewed by Human Resources or IT when needed.

Remote employees are expected to remain available during standard business hours, attend required meetings, respond to messages in a timely manner, and maintain the same productivity expectations as on-site employees.
Employees working remotely must have: - a quiet and professional workspace, - reliable high-speed internet, - access to approved company hardware, - the ability to protect confidential information.

All remote work must comply with the IT Security Policy. Employees must use company-approved devices, strong passwords, multi-factor authentication, and secure connections. Public Wi-Fi may not be used without company-approved VPN protection.
Employees may not regularly work from a different city or country without written approval. Cross-border remote work may create tax, legal, data privacy, and security concerns.

The company may provide a laptop, charger, and approved accessories for business use. Employees are responsible for taking reasonable care of all equipment and reporting damage, loss, or theft immediately.

Remote work is a business privilege, not a guaranteed right. The company may modify or revoke remote work arrangements at any time based on performance, role changes, security issues, or operational needs.
**Company:** Northstar Analytics, Inc. **Version:** 1.0 **Effective Date:** July 1, 2026
The purpose of this policy is to provide employees with planned time away from work to rest, manage personal commitments, and maintain work-life balance.
This policy applies to all full-time employees. Part-time employees may receive prorated vacation benefits if approved by Human Resources and permitted by local law.

Full-time employees receive paid vacation based on length of service: - **0 to 2 years of service:** 15 vacation days per year - **3 to 5 years of service:** 20 vacation days per year - **6 or more years of service:** 25 vacation days per year Vacation is accrued monthly and becomes available as it is earned.

Employees should submit vacation requests through the company HR or scheduling system at least: - **2 weeks in advance** for absences of 1 to 3 days - **4 weeks in advance** for absences longer than 3 days Managers should review requests based on staffing needs, project deadlines, and fairness across the team.

Vacation is not considered approved until the employee receives confirmation from their direct manager or the HR system. Employees should not make non-refundable travel arrangements until leave has been approved.
Employees may carry over up to **5 unused vacation days** into the next calendar year. Any unused days beyond that limit are forfeited unless local law requires a different approach.
Certain business-critical periods may be designated as vacation blackout periods for specific teams. Managers must communicate such periods in advance whenever possible.
Vacation time is intended for planned personal time off. Employees who are ill should use the appropriate sick leave policy or notify management according to absence procedures.
Unused accrued vacation may be paid out at separation if required by law or company policy. Any advanced but unearned vacation may be deducted from final pay where legally allowed.
Questions about accrual balances, approvals, or carryover should be directed to Human Resources.
